behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real git rename detection plus CLI/report wiring. A mock diff can miss real rename status formats/threshold behavior from git subprocess output, so MOCK-HIDE applies. No AI/ML output and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, perform a rename (`git mv`), run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows the file only under `renamed` with no corresponding `added`/`deleted` entries.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures often arise from real git subprocess output and real filesystem binary content; stubbing text diffs can hide crash conditions. This behavior is infra-bound (REAL-INFRA) but not LLM-dependent and not multi-step agentic.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, and assert binary file contributes to report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real `git` behavior in an unborn-HEAD repository (subprocess exit/status semantics). In-process mocks may not reproduce that exact failure mode. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI step.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh repo with zero commits, run CLI subprocess from that repo, assert it exits cleanly and prints the empty structured report format.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly compares report counts against actual repository history, which is produced by real git history traversal and CLI integration. Mocking history can hide real parsing/revision-selection failures. No LLM output dependence and no agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with controlled commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run CLI subprocess, and assert each category count equals ground truth computed from the known commit sequence.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This is a git index vs working-tree state behavior that relies on real git state transitions and subprocess semantics; module-only tests are insufficient and can miss real discrepancies (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is non-AI and single-flow.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same content delta in two setups (staged-only and unstaged-only), run CLI subprocess for each, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
